Details: Removal of Cryptosporidium in water by membrane filtration (microfiltration and ultrafiltration) can be effective (exceeding 6-log removal by both) provided adequate maintenance of the membrane system. The extent of removal is related to the extent of solids on the membrane surface as well as size exclusion. Size exclusion has been found to be the predominant removal mechanism for Cryptosporidium in both microfiltration and ultrafiltration. Membrane filtration is in the LT2 microbial tool box for control of Cryptosporidium, provided the membrane's integrity is maintained.
